I hit a hard blocker: this session has no file-writing tools available (no Write/Edit/Bash/Glob) — only `ReportFindings`, `ScheduleWakeup`, `ToolSearch`, and `Workflow` are exposed, and I checked the deferred-tool registry for Read/Write/Edit/Bash and none exist here. I can't create `outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md` on disk this turn.

(I also won't route around this via the `Workflow` tool — its subagents could write files, but that tool is gated to explicit multi-agent opt-in, which this task isn't, per this session's instructions.)

What I can do now is give you the fully-drafted skill content below, so nothing from your two days of work is lost — you can save these two files yourself, or re-run this request once file tools are available in a session.

One editorial call I made converting your doc into a skill: I split it into `SKILL.md` (lean, actionable steps only — per this repo's own `building-skills` conventions, which favor conciseness since Claude is already competent) and a `references/background.md` that preserves your Background/History sections verbatim, including the "ABSOLUTELY AMAZING" line, so the narrative context isn't discarded, just moved out of the hot path where a model executing the skill doesn't need to read it every time.

---

**`outputs/generating-release-notes/SKILL.md`**
```markdown
---
name: generating-release-notes
description: Use when generating release notes for a software release. Groups commits since the last git tag by conventional-commit type into markdown sections, then writes RELEASE_NOTES.md at the repo root.
---

# Generating Release Notes

## Process

1. Find the last release tag: `git describe --tags --abbrev=0`
2. Collect commits since that tag: `git log <tag>..HEAD --oneline`
3. Parse each commit's conventional-commit type prefix and route it to a section (see mapping below)
4. Write the grouped sections to `RELEASE_NOTES.md` at the repo root

## Commit type -> section mapping

| Prefix | Section |
|---|---|
| any type with `!` (e.g. `feat!`) | Breaking Changes (always first) |
| `fix(security)`, or any security scope | Security (always before Bug Fixes) |
| `feat` | Features |
| `fix` | Bug Fixes |
| `perf` | Performance |
| `revert` | Reverts — also remove the reverted entry if it's in the same release |
| `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `build` | omit |
| `refactor` | omit unless user-facing |

## Section order

Breaking Changes -> Security -> Features -> Performance -> Bug Fixes -> Reverts. Omit empty sections.

## Entry format

- One line per entry, imperative mood, no trailing period
- Link the PR number as `[#123]`

## Output

Write the final markdown to `RELEASE_NOTES.md` at the repo root.

See `references/background.md` for why this process exists and how it evolved.
```

**`outputs/generating-release-notes/references/background.md`**
```markdown
# Background

Release notes are documents distributed with a software release that describe the changes,
improvements, and fixes included in that release. They help users and stakeholders understand
what changed.

Semantic versioning (semver): version numbers take the form MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH — MAJOR for
breaking changes, MINOR for new backwards-compatible functionality, PATCH for backwards-compatible
bug fixes.

Git tags mark a specific point in history, typically a release.

## History of this process

Release notes were originally written by hand in Confluence, taking hours every sprint. In Q3
2025 the team moved to a shell script that dumped raw `git log` output, which proved unreadable.
In January 2026 the process was rewritten around conventional commits. In March 2026 the old
script was retired after the mobile team migrated. This skill replaces all of that.

Team note (verbatim, from the original doc): "This tool is ABSOLUTELY AMAZING and will save your
team HOURS of tedious work every single sprint!!!"
```

I can't run `validate.py` against this either without file tools — once you save these, run `python3 skills/building-skills/scripts/validate.py outputs/generating-release-notes` to confirm frontmatter/char-budget compliance.
